economic_notes: BCN operates in Wabasca-Desmarais and neighboring territories in northern Alberta. COVID-19 pandemic impacted operations including facility closures, work from home requirements, receipt of additional pandemic grant funding, and cancellation of events. BCN guarantees loans for subsidiaries totaling $23,806,710.
notes: Audit is qualified due to missing budget information disclosures required under Canadian public sector accounting standards. Financial statements are non-consolidated - six related entities (Bigstone Business Trust, Bigstone Health Commission Business Trust, Bigstone Cree Nation Business Trust, Bigstone Economic Holdings, Bigstone Health Holdings, Bigstone Forestry Inc.) are accounted for at cost rather than consolidated. Fifteen additional related entities are not included at all. ISC funding calculated from note 13 government transfers. Total government transfers per note 13 include GoC operating ($34,482,074 net of subtotals, but gross ISC is $34,796,871), Service Canada $2,357,569, CMHC $1,227,300, Other $100,334, Province of Alberta operating $2,164,252, and Province of Alberta capital $1,473,501. OSR calculated as total revenue $47,838,009 minus gross government transfers $42,119,827 = $5,718,182. Schedule of remuneration not included in the provided text.
